overview: The Village of Weston Community Life and Public Safety Committee met on May 4th, approving amendments to the nuisance ordinance for recommendation to the board, advancing an e-bike ordinance with added emoto restrictions, and authorizing a raze order for a dilapidated structure at 4104 Shore Avenue. The committee also discussed overnight parking regulations and a crosswalk request, deferring both items for further staff work.
committee: Community Life and Public Safety Committee

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Approval of minutes from previous meeting
Acknowledgment of department reports (Code Enforcement, Fire/EMS, Police)
Mobile food vendors fire inspection requirements

discussions.body: Jim Senalt addressed the committee with concerns about the new farmers market location at Kennedy Park, stating 'my personal opinion is it's a terrible execution' and predicting the grassy areas will be destroyed. He raised concerns about ADA-compliant parking being placed on grass rather than asphalt, questioned the necessity of new fire inspections for food trucks, and criticized the age of some code enforcement cases, noting some have been pending for years with no visible progress.

Roman/Audrey reported that 3020 Mountain View Avenue will be removed from the code enforcement roll after the property sale was completed and the new owner submitted repair permits. Chief Hunt noted that the police department had 2,394 calls in March (77 per day), 410 traffic stops, and 281 citations. The department has hired its 40th officer, with the 38th starting last week, 39th starting in May, and 40th around January. Two SROs work summer zoning violations across three municipalities. Motion to acknowledge reports 5, 6, and 7 made by Chwanka, passed unanimously.

Committee reviewed final copy of nuisance ordinance amendments, including previously discussed changes to tree limb height requirements (14 and 10 feet over streets and walkways). Motion by Olsen, seconded by Campkey, to recommend approval to the Board of Trustees passed unanimously.

Committee extensively discussed the proposed e-bike ordinance for Chapter 82. Chief Hunt strongly advocated for prohibiting emotos on roadways and sidewalks, comparing them to unlicensed dirt bikes. Discussion covered whether electric scooters should be included in restrictions, with committee members noting middle schoolers commonly use them. After debate about over-regulation versus safety, motion by Olsen, seconded by Chwanka, to recommend approval with the addition of prohibiting emotos on roadways, sidewalks, trails, and public property passed unanimously.

Trustee Tolson presented research showing Weston's 12-hour parking restriction window (4am-4pm) is significantly longer than surrounding municipalities (most have 5-hour windows). Michael from BPW noted overnight restrictions don't help operations since plowing rarely occurs before 4-5am. Chief Hunt advocated keeping overnight restrictions to give officers time to clear vehicles before morning plowing. Committee discussed uniformity concerns and impacts on multifamily housing. Item deferred to July meeting for Michael to identify specific problem areas that need signage.

Committee discussed Mount Olive Church's request for a crosswalk between the church and DC Everest school parking lot. Staff estimated cost at $2,000-3,000 for basic crosswalk, $25,000-30,000 for lighted system. Committee members expressed reluctance to set precedent of paying for requested crosswalks. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Chwanka, to recommend board approval contingent on Mount Olive covering installation costs, passed unanimously.

Chief Hunt explained a regional consortium (Wisconsin Rapids, Marshfield, Stevens Point, Plover, Rib Mountain, and Safer) has established mutual fire inspection recognition for food trucks. One inspection with a sticker is valid across all participating municipalities. Fire Marshal Marty already conducted one inspection that failed due to improper LP piping and unsecured propane containers. Wausau opted out to do their own inspections. Motion by Chwanka, seconded by Campkey, to approve Ordinance 26-8 passed unanimously.

Staff presented three addressing errors requiring correction for E911 compliance: 5915 Delicoski Street (duplex needing separate addresses), properties on Ross Avenue requiring renumbering due to sequence issues, and 2211 Radkey Avenue needing even number assignment. Chief Hunt also identified Weston Villa Apartments on Delicoski as having unusual addressing with eight separate addresses for one structure. Motion passed unanimously to recommend board approval.

Staff requested authorization for raze order on structure abandoned since 2010-2014. The structure was previously considered for fire department training burn but was too deteriorated for safe exercise. Chickens were found being housed inside when a light was observed in the building. Current owners, who are up to date on taxes, prefer the village handle demolition with costs placed on tax roll. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Campkey, passed unanimously.

publicComment: Jim Senalt spoke during public comment, criticizing the new farmers market location at Kennedy Park as 'terrible execution,' raising concerns about ADA parking compliance on grass, questioning the need for food truck fire inspections, and expressing frustration with the age of some code enforcement cases that have shown no visible progress.
actionItems: Chapter 50 nuisance amendments recommended to Board of Trustees for approval
E-bike ordinance with emoto prohibition added recommended to Board of Trustees
Michael (BPW) to identify specific areas needing parking signage and bring back to July meeting
Michael (BPW) to contact Mount Olive Church regarding cost-sharing for crosswalk installation
Ordinance 26-8 for mobile food vendor fire inspections approved
Address corrections for three properties recommended to Board of Trustees
Raze order for 4104 Shore Avenue authorized
Staff to investigate property at Sternberg and South Timber with furniture and dumpster outside
Next meeting scheduled for Monday, July 7th at 5:00 PM
title: Community Life and Public Safety
